summary: No-ship: the slice can pass its unit test while a normal modes composition does not actually enable souls, and selected-modes failure paths are too silent.

findings.title: Modes activation is gated on an mcp-gateway install path the slice does not make reachable
findings.recommendation: Wire the sidecar into the supported dependency path before gating on it: add an installable/selectable mcp-gateway entry or make it a transitive dependency, ensure resolveGatewayFitting cannot accidentally choose the sidecar as the main HTTP gateway, and add a runner-level test with a real modes composition that proves GARRISON_SOULS_CONFIG reaches spawnGateway.

findings.title: Selected modes can silently fall back to normal gateway mode
findings.recommendation: Do not collapse malformed, missing, or empty modes.json to null once a modes provider was selected. Validate and throw, or have runner.up emit a stderr warning/fail when modesEntry exists but assembleSouls returns null. Adjust the null-path test so it does not bless a silent downgrade.

findings.title: orchestratorFittingId is serialized but not passed in the shape the gateway uses
findings.recommendation: Either set GARRISON_ORCHESTRATOR_FITTING_ID in gatewayExtraEnv or update gateway.mjs to consume soulsConfig.orchestratorFittingId, then add a test that exercises the gateway load/boot path rather than only asserting the JSON object shape.
